MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-15-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT, CROSS-DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT MONTE STOTT & ASSOCIATES, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

APPEARANCES: Mary E. Alexander, Esq. & Jennifer L. Fiore, Esq. of Mary Alexander & Associates, P.C. 433-4440 for pltf & opposing party; William J. Peters, Esq. of Gordon & Rees LLP 875-3143 for moving party Monte Stott & Associates; Gina Dashman Boer, Esq. of Haapala, Thompson & Abern, LLP 510/763-2324 for Rocchiccioli, Scwhacther, Badhuri; Michael P. Clark, Esq. of Law Offices of McNamara, Ney, Beatty, Slattery, Borges & Ambacher LLP 925/939-5330 for Pine Street Homeowners Association; Amy N. Solaro, Esq. of Bledsoe, Cathcart, Diestel, Pedersen & Treppa LLP 981-5411 for Bhaduri.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Motion for summary judgment/summary adjudication denied, failure to shift the burden of proof. No expert declaration or evidence presented regarding the professional standard of care. Triable issues of fact raised whether defendants nonfeasance (breach) was a substantial factor in causing injury. Experts Panasci and Hallet declare that defendant had a duty to provide complete plans that included providing sufficient detail regarding the railings. Mr. Paul stated that Mr. Stott admitted the plans should have more detail regarding the guard rail (depo pp. 105-106). Whether defendant's nonfeasance/defective plans were a substantial factor in causing injury presents a factual issue. It was foreseeable that unprofessional contractors may fill the gaps in the plans in an unsafe manner. Negligence per se is not a cause of action, but an evidentiary presumption that cannot be disposed of under CCP 437 c(f)(1). Although defendant did not own, lease, occupy, or maintain the property, no evidence is presented that defendant cannot be held liable for premises liability by control of the property or the creation of a dangerous condition. Defendant fails to shift the burden of proof. Order signed in open court. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
